seo.title: 100 Developer Tools resources for developers
seo.description: A curated set of practical Developer Tools resources for engineers.

content.intro: A specialized toolkit for developers building developer-facing products, focusing on high-performance CLI frameworks, automated SDK generation, and distribution infrastructure for enterprise-grade devtools.

items.title: oclif
items.description: An extensible framework for building CLIs in Node.js, featuring automatic documentation generation and plugin support used by Heroku and Salesforce.

items.title: Ink
items.description: React-based library for building interactive CLI layouts using components; ideal for complex terminal dashboards and progress indicators.

items.title: Clap (Rust)
items.description: The industry-standard argument parser for Rust, providing high-speed execution and comprehensive validation for CLI inputs.

items.title: Charm Lip Gloss
items.description: A Go library for defining terminal styles via a declarative API, essential for building aesthetically pleasing TUI applications.

items.title: Commander.js
items.description: The most widely used Node.js CLI library; use this for lightweight tools where minimal bundle size and broad compatibility are required.

items.title: Zod
items.description: TypeScript-first schema validation; use it to validate CLI configuration files (e.g., .toolrc) at runtime to prevent silent failures.

items.title: Conf
items.description: Simple library for handling persistent configuration and local settings for CLI tools, abstracting away OS-specific file paths.

items.title: Ora
items.description: The standard terminal spinner for Node.js; use to provide visual feedback during long-running async tasks like dependency installation.

items.title: Enquirer
items.description: User-friendly and accessible terminal prompts for gathering user input, supporting autocomplete and multi-select modes.

items.title: Chalk
items.description: Terminal string styling library; implement this to ensure error messages are high-contrast (red) and success messages are clear (green).

items.title: Speakeasy
items.description: Automated SDK generation platform that produces idiomatic libraries in multiple languages from a single OpenAPI specification.

items.title: Fern
items.description: Define your API using YAML and generate high-quality SDKs and documentation; includes built-in support for multiple programming languages.

items.title: Spectral
items.description: A flexible JSON/YAML linter; use it to enforce API design standards and ensure OpenAPI files remain valid and consistent.

items.title: Scalar
items.description: Interactive API documentation that generates code snippets and allows developers to test endpoints directly from the browser.

items.title: Prism
items.description: API mock server that uses OpenAPI documents to provide realistic responses during development without a backend implementation.

items.title: Zod-to-OpenAPI
items.description: Library to generate OpenAPI 3.x documentation from Zod schemas, ensuring your code and documentation stay in sync.

items.title: Liblab
items.description: SDK-as-a-service that handles the generation, maintenance, and distribution of client libraries for your API products.

items.title: Buf
items.description: Modern tooling for Protocol Buffers; use this to manage, lint, and distribute gRPC-based developer tools and APIs.

items.title: Hono
items.description: Ultrafast web framework for the Edges; use this for building devtool backends that require minimal latency and zero cold starts.

items.title: Postman Collections
items.description: Exportable API suites that allow users to import your entire API into their workspace for immediate testing and exploration.

items.title: Polar.sh
items.description: Monetization platform for open-source developers allowing you to offer 'pay-per-feature' or subscription tiers for devtools.

items.title: GoReleaser
items.description: Automates the delivery of Go binaries to GitHub, Homebrew, and Scoop, handling cross-compilation and checksum generation.

items.title: Keygen.sh
items.description: Software licensing and activation API designed specifically for desktop applications, CLI tools, and private SDKs.

items.title: Homebrew Tap
items.description: Creating a custom Homebrew repository allows macOS and Linux users to install your CLI tool via a simple 'brew install' command.

items.title: NPM Provenance
items.description: Enables verifiable links between NPM packages and their source code/CI build, increasing trust for security-conscious developers.

items.title: Cloudsmith
items.description: Cloud-native package management for private repositories, useful for distributing enterprise-only versions of SDKs or plugins.

items.title: Stripe Billing
items.description: Use Stripe's metered billing features to implement usage-based pricing for API-driven developer tools.

items.title: Pkg
items.description: Packages Node.js projects into single executable files for multiple platforms, allowing users to run tools without Node installed.

items.title: Segment
items.description: Integrate Segment into your CLI or SDK to track feature usage and identify which parts of your tool are most adopted.

items.title: Vercel SDK
items.description: Utilize Vercel's edge runtime for deploying devtool dashboards and documentation sites with global low latency.

pro_tips: Always implement a --json flag in CLI tools to allow users to pipe output into jq for automation workflows.
Use the XDG Base Directory Specification to store configuration and cache files in standard system locations (e.g., ~/.config).
Detect if process.stdout.isTTY is false to automatically disable animations and colors when the tool is run in a CI/CD environment.
Leverage Tree-sitter for syntax analysis in IDE extensions instead of regex for more robust and performant code manipulation.
Include a global --help flag and ensure it describes every sub-command and argument to reduce onboarding friction for new users.
